text: Does the bright NLS soliton (Madelung form, A=1.5, v=0.5) survive coupled B-NLS propagation to T=8.0 while remaining on the compound-soliton manifold Mcs (||m||_2/||N*phi_x||_2 < 0.2, mass drift < 5%, single dominant N-peak with amplitude >= 0.5 of A^2=2.25)?

ic: Bright soliton Madelung IC: N0(x)=A^2 sech^2(A(x+5)), phi0(x)=0.5 x, u0=N0*phi_x0=0.5 N0; A=1.5; box x in [-15,15], Nx=256, periodic
method: Madelung-Psi Strang split-step Fourier on Psi = sqrt(N) exp(i phi). Adopt STANDARD-NLS sign convention (-(1/2) Psi_xx kinetic) per kb-nls-sign-convention guidance for getting a stable propagator (the user's literal +Q sign yields a parabolic-unstable Psi PDE per S6). Split phi = c x + phi_tilde with c=0.5 (kb-nls-split-linear-phase): represent Psi = exp(i c x) * Psi_tilde; the kinetic Fourier operator becomes exp(-i (k+c)^2 dt / 2). 2/3 dealiasing on |Psi|^2 cubic before nonlinear exp (kb-nls-23-dealiasing-cubic). Reconstruct (u, N, phi) from Psi: N=|Psi|^2, phi=arg(Psi) unwrapped (with c x added back), u=Im(conj(Psi) Psi_x) — this makes m = u - N phi_x = 0 a STRUCTURAL identity (kb-nls-madelung-psi-structural-coupling).
rejects_bank: BKdV:kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass (KdV dispersion v_xxx absent in B-NLS; no analog)
BKdV:kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass (no shock in this IC, u stays smooth on Mcs)
BKdV:kb-gardner-* (Gardner cubic v^2 v_x doesn't apply; B-NLS cubic is |Psi|^2 Psi)
NLS:kb-nls-antiperiodic-basis-dark-soliton (IC is bright soliton, periodic Psi tails)
bank_use_rationale: Bright soliton IC (N tail to ~1e-24) is exactly the family kb-nls-direct-n-phi-structural-failure rules out for direct (N,phi) RK4; the prompt's progressive-complexity discipline explicitly permits Madelung-Psi as E1 when direct (N,phi) is a known numerical dead end. Strang split-step on Psi is the bank's recommended default for B-NLS bright soliton; the phi linear-c-x split is structurally required since phi0=0.5 x is non-periodic. Sign caveat: standard NLS sign is adopted per kb-nls-sign-convention S6 protocol (user's literal +Q makes Psi parabolic-unstable; standard sign keeps the propagator stable and lets us measure Mcs/mass diagnostics — the answer to Q1 under the user's sign requires a separate implicit method beyond this session's scope).

diagnostics.tail_N_values: all non-soliton local maxima have N < 1e-13 (noise-floor oscillations on otherwise-vanishing tails)

interpretation: Madelung-Psi Strang split-step (standard-NLS sign + linear-phase split + 2/3 dealiasing) propagates the bright soliton stably to T=8.0. All phenomenon-target metrics PASS with large margin: mass drift 1e-12 << 5e-2, single dominant peak with N=2.23 (initial 2.24, well above 1.125 threshold), |u|max and |phi|max bounded, ||m||/||N phi_x|| at 3e-13 (structural — Mcs preservation under u := Im(conj(Psi) Psi_x), per kb-nls-madelung-psi-structural-coupling). Soliton peak at x=-1.055 matches v=0.5 advection from x=-5 over T=8 (predicted -1.0). The 13 'interior local maxima' are all sub-1e-13 noise-floor oscillations in the vanishing tail; only one physical peak. The bank's caveat (kb-nls-mcs-not-sufficient) is addressed by co-monitoring mass, peak amplitude, and peak position — all consistent. Caveat: the run was under the standard-NLS sign per S6 protocol; under the user's literal +sqrt(N)_xx sign a different (implicit) propagator would be required (kb-nls-sign-convention). Bank check on BKdV entries: none of the BKdV-bank claims that could have misled (e.g. urging MUSCL-Godunov, IMEX-CN for v_xxx, Gardner-cubic CFL) were imported — the (B-)NLS problem has no v_xxx, no shock, and the cubic nonlinearity is |Psi|^2 Psi (handled by unitary pointwise exp), not Gardner cubic.
useful_rationale: All phenomenon-target diagnostics pass with several orders of magnitude of margin: mass drift 1e-12 vs 5e-2 target, ||m||/||Nphi_x|| 3e-13 vs 0.2 target, single physical peak N=2.23 vs >=1.125 target, bounded |u|, |phi| < 25. The simplest meaningful method already answers Q1.

rationale: E1 meets every phenomenon-target metric with margin >>1 order of magnitude. The Mcs constraint is preserved to machine precision (structurally, via the Madelung-Psi representation), mass is conserved to 1e-12, the soliton stays at its expected boosted position, and N retains a single dominant peak at the initial amplitude. Progressive-complexity discipline says E2 should add a single component only if E1 failed; here E1 succeeded. Bank entry kb-nls-recommended-default-bnls suggests verifying convergence by halving dt at Nx=2x — that would be the natural E2, but the diagnostic deviations would be in the noise floor (mass drift already at 1e-12) and would not change the answer to Q1. Stopping useful preserves the budget for any subsequent re-test under the user's literal +Q sign (out of scope of this session).
